response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a broad background across TypeScript, Node.js, and some embedded systems exposure, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the hard requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is entirely absent from his profile — he lists only English, Romanian, and German. Beyond the language barrier, his career has been oriented toward web platforms, APIs, and mobile applications rather than embedded firmware, network security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), or Linux kernel development. His C/C++ experience is minimal and dated, appearing only in a student thesis and a brief NI LabWindows role over a decade ago, with no evidence of the systems-level security or networking domain knowledge this position requires.
strengths: Over 15 years of professional software development experience across diverse environments, demonstrating strong engineering fundamentals and adaptability.

Has touched embedded systems and low-latency application development (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic, robotics at Altom, student thesis in Linux/C++), showing some foundational systems-level exposure.
Solid Linux familiarity and experience with REST APIs, CI/CD pipelines, and open-source project ownership.
concerns: Completely lacks Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role.
No experience with any of the required security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication, encryption key exchange) or embedded networking/switch products.
C programming is not a demonstrated core skill; the role demands strong C proficiency for UNIX/embedded firmware, while his stack is overwhelmingly TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented.

key_factors.factor: No security or embedded networking domain experience
